title: helmet({ contentSecurityPolicy: false }) — Content-Security-Policy is turned off, removing the main XSS mitigation

params.passDetail: helmet keeps Content-Security-Policy on (contentSecurityPolicy is not false) — a CSP header is sent that constrains where scripts and resources can load from.
params.failDetail: helmet is configured with contentSecurityPolicy: false, which DISABLES the Content-Security-Policy header entirely. CSP is the primary defense-in-depth against XSS and data injection; with it off, any injected script runs unrestricted and can exfiltrate tokens or data. Leave CSP enabled (helmet's default) and configure directives instead of turning it off.
params.absentCallDetail: No helmet() call in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: helmet() does not set contentSecurityPolicy; the default (CSP enabled) applies.

provenance.note: contentSecurityPolicy: false is pasted to stop CSP from blocking an inline script during development and left in. The app works, so it ships — silently removing the strongest XSS mitigation the stack had.
